reason: MIT attribution must preserve the upstream copyright text.
retirementRule: Retain while any OpenCode-derived source remains in the repository or release artifacts.

reason: Public docs and package notices explicitly describe OpenCode-derived attribution.
retirementRule: Retire only if the repository no longer contains OpenCode-derived source.

reason: The validator and its allowlist intentionally encode reviewed OpenCode references and categories.
retirementRule: Retire only if the validator is removed or renamed away from the upstream terminology.

reason: Governance metadata references OpenCode-derived attribution and upstream-candidate issue routing.
retirementRule: Retire if GitHub metadata no longer references upstream lineage or candidate routing.

reason: Repository metadata, workspace scripts, overlay authority, and migration plans still reference the current host path or upstream schema URL.
retirementRule: Update or retire when the host directory rename and remaining schema migrations are complete.

reason: Agent instructions preserve upstream attribution and the current host path during the split.
retirementRule: Revisit after the host directory rename and any AGENTS content refresh.

reason: Nested AGENTS guides preserve upstream attribution and legacy fixture naming while the host split is in progress.
retirementRule: Retire or narrow after the host directory rename and fixture naming cleanup.

reason: Upstream package names must stay intact for dependency resolution.
retirementRule: Retire only when the dependency or import name changes upstream.

reason: The built-in theme asset keeps its upstream file name for compatibility with the existing theme registry.
retirementRule: Retire if the theme asset is renamed and the theme registry is updated.

reason: Theme asset schema URLs still point at the upstream schema authority.
retirementRule: Retire when the theme schema URL moves to an Interbase-owned authority.

reason: This internal API symbol remains upstream-shaped during the host split.
retirementRule: Retire when the internal HTTP API symbol is renamed without compatibility risk.

reason: Source comments describe upstream sync context or legacy config semantics.
retirementRule: Retire when those comments are no longer needed for sync or migration context.

reason: Prompt templates and tool guidance intentionally preserve upstream wording and docs URLs pending a separate prompt-branding pass.
retirementRule: Retire or narrow when prompt and tool templates adopt the Interbase wording contract.

reason: The committed provider snapshot may contain upstream-generated provider identifiers and URLs.
retirementRule: Regenerate and narrow when the snapshot no longer carries upstream naming fragments.

reason: Design specs, developer READMEs, and templates intentionally describe current upstream paths, legacy commands, and migration semantics.
retirementRule: Retire or narrow as the host split, docs, and templates stop referencing upstream names and paths.

reason: Fixture data intentionally preserves upstream provider and docs identifiers for regression coverage.
retirementRule: Retire or narrow when fixture coverage no longer depends on upstream identifiers.

reason: Generated SDK artifacts still contain upstream-generated naming and descriptions.
retirementRule: Regenerate and narrow the allowlist as the generated public contract adopts Interbase branding.

reason: These scripts intentionally reference the current host path or legacy local binary naming.
retirementRule: Update or retire when the host directory rename completes.

reason: Tests intentionally exercise legacy names, host paths, and migration scenarios.
retirementRule: Narrow or remove once tests stop covering legacy names and current host paths.
